exercises.title: Identify breaking vs non-breaking API changes
exercises.instructions:
Razorpay changes payment API. Flipkart's consumer uses: paymentId, status, amountPaise.
Which changes BREAK the Pact contract?
A: Add new field 'gateway' to response (Flipkart doesn't use it)
B: Rename 'paymentId' → 'id'
C: Change 'amountPaise' type from Long to String
D: Add optional 'merchantName' field
E: Remove 'status' field
F: Change status values from 'SUCCESS' to 'COMPLETED'
exercises.expected_solution:
BREAKING (Pact contract violated):
  B: Rename 'paymentId' → 'id'
     Flipkart's consumer expects 'paymentId' field
     Pact: stringType('paymentId') → field missing → VIOLATION
     Flipkart code: response.paymentId() → NullPointerException

  C: Change 'amountPaise' from Long to String
     Pact: numberType('amountPaise') → got String type → VIOLATION
     Flipkart code: long amount = response.amountPaise() → ClassCastException

  E: Remove 'status' field
     Flipkart expects 'status' field in response
     Pact: stringType('status') → field missing → VIOLATION
     Flipkart: null status → NPE or wrong logic

  F: Change status 'SUCCESS' → 'COMPLETED'
     IF Flipkart used stringMatcher('status', 'SUCCESS|PENDING'): VIOLATION
     IF Flipkart used stringType('status'): NON-BREAKING (any string accepted)
     Depends on matcher strictness in consumer test

NON-BREAKING:
  A: Add 'gateway' field (Flipkart doesn't use it → not in contract → ignored)
  D: Add optional 'merchantName' field (same — not in Flipkart's contract)
     New fields don't break existing consumers' contracts

test_cases.expected:
BREAKING (Pact contract violated):
  B: Rename 'paymentId' → 'id'
     Flipkart's consumer expects 'paymentId' field
     Pact: stringType('paymentId') → field missing → VIOLATION
     Flipkart code: response.paymentId() → NullPointerException

  C: Change 'amountPaise' from Long to String
     Pact: numberType('amountPaise') → got String type → VIOLATION
     Flipkart code: long amount = response.amountPaise() → ClassCastException

  E: Remove 'status' field
     Flipkart expects 'status' field in response
     Pact: stringType('status') → field missing → VIOLATION
     Flipkart: null status → NPE or wrong logic

  F: Change status 'SUCCESS' → 'COMPLETED'
     IF Flipkart used stringMatcher('status', 'SUCCESS|PENDING'): VIOLATION
     IF Flipkart used stringType('status'): NON-BREAKING (any string accepted)
     Depends on matcher strictness in consumer test

NON-BREAKING:
  A: Add 'gateway' field (Flipkart doesn't use it → not in contract → ignored)
  D: Add optional 'merchantName' field (same — not in Flipkart's contract)
     New fields don't break existing consumers' contracts

hints: Consumer contract: only fields consumer actually uses
Add new fields: safe (backward compatible)
Remove/rename/retype existing fields: breaking

exercises.title: Complete the Pact consumer test
exercises.instructions: Fill in the Pact consumer test structure.
exercises.starter_code:
@___(providerName="razorpay-api")
public ___ createPact(___ builder) {
    return builder
        .given("MRC-001 exists")
        .uponReceiving("create payment")
            .method("POST").path("/api/payments")
        .willRespondWith()
            .status(201)
            .body(new PactDslJsonBody()
                .___Type("paymentId"))
        .___()
}

blanks.hint: Pact annotation linking consumer test to a provider
Pact type for HTTP request-response contracts
DSL builder for defining request-response interactions
matcher that checks field is present and is a String type
terminal builder method that generates the contract

hints: @PactTestFor: links the test method to a specific pact provider
RequestResponsePact: the contract object generated by the @Pact method
toPact(): terminal method — builds and returns the contract

exercises.title: Pact consumer test — Flipkart calling Razorpay
exercises.instructions:
Write minimal Pact consumer test:
1. @Pact: POST /api/payments → 201 with {paymentId:String, status:String}
2. @Test: PaymentClient calls mock server, verifies fields present
3. No real Razorpay needed (Pact starts mock server)
4. @ExtendWith(PactConsumerTestExt.class): test runs with Pact JUnit 5 extension
hints: MockServer: Pact starts this automatically based on your @Pact definition
stringType('paymentId', 'PAY-001'): checks it's a String (example value for the mock response)
@PactTestFor(pactMethod='createPaymentPact'): links @Test to the @Pact method

exercises.title: PactDslJsonBody matchers — type vs value
exercises.instructions:
Show the difference between Pact matchers:
1. stringType vs stringValue (exact match)
2. numberType vs integerType
3. stringMatcher (regex)
4. minArrayLike (array with at least N items)
5. @SpringBootTest: demonstrate matcher behavior in a simple test
hints: stringType: any String (flexible). stringValue: exact match (brittle)
stringMatcher: regex — perfect for enum-like status fields
minArrayLike: verifies array structure — at least N items of specified shape

exercises.title: Pact provider verification
exercises.instructions:
Write Pact provider test for Razorpay payment API:
1. @SpringBootTest(webEnvironment=RANDOM_PORT)
2. @Provider + @PactFolder (reads local pact JSON files)
3. @BeforeEach: set target to localhost:port
4. @State: setup data for 'merchant MRC-001 exists'
5. @TestTemplate: runs ctx.verifyInteraction()

expected_stdout_contains: Pact provider: reads consumer contract, verifies real API matches

hints: @Provider: must match provider name in @Pact on consumer side (exact string)
@PactFolder: reads pact JSON files from local directory (for local testing)
@State: string must EXACTLY match consumer's .given() — copy-paste to be safe

exercises.title: Pact with Pact Broker publication
exercises.instructions:
Show how to publish pacts to Pact Broker and use can-i-deploy:
1. Maven pact-jvm-provider plugin config (publish)
2. @PactBroker annotation for provider test
3. can-i-deploy command for CI gate
4. @SpringBootTest: demonstrate the workflow in code
hints: can-i-deploy: Pact Broker CLI tool — checks if all consumer contracts are verified
tags: link pact versions to git branches (main, feature/x)
@PactBroker vs @PactFolder: Broker for CI, PactFolder for local

exercises.title: Contract test for GET payment endpoint
exercises.instructions:
Write consumer Pact test for GET /api/payments/{paymentId}:
1. Consumer (Flipkart) expects: paymentId, status, amountPaise, merchantId, createdAt (ISO date)
2. PactDslJsonBody with appropriate matchers
3. @Test: GET returns all expected fields
4. @SpringBootTest: consumer test generates contract
hints: datetime matcher: validates ISO 8601 format — important for date fields
stringMatcher for status: validates against allowed values (regex)
GET path: use exact path /api/payments/PAY-001 (or path regex for dynamic IDs)

exercises.title: Multiple consumers for same provider
hints: Multiple consumer tests: each in its own class with different port
Provider must return all fields any consumer needs
Removing 'gateway': Zomato pact breaks, Flipkart pact passes → can-i-deploy FAIL

exercises.title: Contract test for error responses
hints: stringValue for error codes: exact match (enums should not change)
404 contract: tells provider exactly what error shape consumers expect
Test error scenarios: contract tests should cover success AND error paths

exercises.title: Contract test failure analysis
exercises.instructions:
Flipkart's pact for Razorpay's payment API:
```java
.body(new PactDslJsonBody()
    .stringType('paymentId')
    .numberType('amountPaise')
    .stringMatcher('status','SUCCESS|FAILED','SUCCESS'))
```

Razorpay's actual API returns:
```json
{ "id": "PAY-001", "amount": 5000.0, "state": "COMPLETED", "currency": "INR" }
```

1. Which contract assertions fail?
2. Which assertions pass?
3. What error message would Pact show?
4. What must Razorpay fix to satisfy the contract?
exercises.expected_solution:
1. Contract assertions that FAIL:
   a) stringType('paymentId') → FAIL
      Field 'paymentId' missing from response (API returns 'id')
      Pact: 'Expected 'paymentId' but got null'
   
   b) numberType('amountPaise') → FAIL
      Field 'amountPaise' missing (API returns 'amount')
      Even though 'amount' is a number, the field NAME must match
   
   c) stringMatcher('status','SUCCESS|FAILED') → FAIL
      Field 'status' missing (API returns 'state')
      AND 'COMPLETED' doesn't match regex 'SUCCESS|FAILED'
      Two violations: wrong field name + wrong value format

2. Assertions that PASS:
   None of the consumer assertions pass (all 3 fail)
   'currency' field: not in consumer contract → ignored (irrelevant)

3. Pact error message (approximate):
   '1) Verifying a pact between flipkart and razorpay-payment-api'
   '   Expected 'paymentId' (String) but was missing from response'
   '   Expected 'amountPaise' (Number) but was missing from response'
   '   Expected 'status' to match [SUCCESS|FAILED] but was missing'
   'There were 3 pact failures'

4. What Razorpay must fix:
   Option A (backward compatible — preferred):
     Return BOTH old and new field names during transition:
     { 'paymentId': 'PAY-001', 'id': 'PAY-001',
       'amountPaise': 5000, 'amount': 5000,
       'status': 'SUCCESS', 'state': 'COMPLETED' }
     → satisfies Flipkart (paymentId, amountPaise, status=SUCCESS)
     → allows new consumers to use new names
     → deprecate old names over time
   
   Option B (breaking):
     Update Flipkart's consumer test to use new field names
     Risk: coordination required across teams

test_cases.expected:
1. Contract assertions that FAIL:
   a) stringType('paymentId') → FAIL
      Field 'paymentId' missing from response (API returns 'id')
      Pact: 'Expected 'paymentId' but got null'
   
   b) numberType('amountPaise') → FAIL
      Field 'amountPaise' missing (API returns 'amount')
      Even though 'amount' is a number, the field NAME must match
   
   c) stringMatcher('status','SUCCESS|FAILED') → FAIL
      Field 'status' missing (API returns 'state')
      AND 'COMPLETED' doesn't match regex 'SUCCESS|FAILED'
      Two violations: wrong field name + wrong value format

2. Assertions that PASS:
   None of the consumer assertions pass (all 3 fail)
   'currency' field: not in consumer contract → ignored (irrelevant)

3. Pact error message (approximate):
   '1) Verifying a pact between flipkart and razorpay-payment-api'
   '   Expected 'paymentId' (String) but was missing from response'
   '   Expected 'amountPaise' (Number) but was missing from response'
   '   Expected 'status' to match [SUCCESS|FAILED] but was missing'
   'There were 3 pact failures'

4. What Razorpay must fix:
   Option A (backward compatible — preferred):
     Return BOTH old and new field names during transition:
     { 'paymentId': 'PAY-001', 'id': 'PAY-001',
       'amountPaise': 5000, 'amount': 5000,
       'status': 'SUCCESS', 'state': 'COMPLETED' }
     → satisfies Flipkart (paymentId, amountPaise, status=SUCCESS)
     → allows new consumers to use new names
     → deprecate old names over time
   
   Option B (breaking):
     Update Flipkart's consumer test to use new field names
     Risk: coordination required across teams

hints: All 3 fields fail: field names changed (paymentId→id, amountPaise→amount, status→state)
Extra fields (currency): ignored by Pact — not in consumer contract
Fix: dual-write both old and new field names during migration

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay consumer-provider pact
hints: PactStates constants: prevents @State vs .given() mismatches
Different ports (8085, 8086): each consumer test needs its own mock server port
Provider must return: {paymentId, status, amountPaise, gateway} to satisfy BOTH consumers

exercises.title: Contract test for Kafka message schema
exercises.instructions:
Extend Pact to Kafka message contracts (Pact MessagePact):
1. Consumer (InventoryService) expects PaymentCompletedEvent: {paymentId, merchantId, amountPaise}
2. @MessagePact: define expected message schema
3. @Test: consumer handler receives and processes correctly
4. @SpringBootTest: message contract verified
hints: MessagePact: for async/event-driven contracts (Kafka, RabbitMQ, SNS)
Pact generates mock message: no real Kafka needed for consumer test
providerType=V3: required for message pacts

exercises.title: Complete multi-consumer pact suite
exercises.instructions:
Build complete multi-consumer pact suite:
1. 3 consumers: Flipkart (create payment), Zomato (get payment), HDFC (webhook event)
2. State constants in PactStates class
3. Each consumer test class independently generates a pact
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): all 3 consumers run
5. All 3 pass
hints: Each consumer: different class, different port
PactStates constants: shared across test classes
Provider must satisfy: paymentId + status + amountPaise + gateway + webhook received

exercises.title: Pact breaking change detection in CI
hints: V1 pact itself passes (consumer test always uses mock server)
Breaking change: detected at PROVIDER verification time (not consumer test)
assertFalse(contains('paymentId')): simulates what provider CI check does

hints: Different ports (8091, 8092): each consumer test class needs unique port
